mnemonics: Suspend Signals Until Safe: SUSPend waits for a SIGnal to reSUMe, ensuring the signal mask is set correctly first.
keyPoints: sigsuspend is used to atomically unblock signals and suspend the process until a signal is caught.
It temporarily replaces the process's signal mask with the one provided as an argument for the duration of the call.
It always returns -1 with errno set to EINTR, as it is interrupted by a signal handler.
commonMistakes: Assuming sigsuspend returns a value other than -1; it always returns -1 after being interrupted.
Using a non-atomic sequence of sigprocmask and pause instead of sigsuspend, creating a race condition.
Forgetting that the provided signal mask is used only during the suspension; the original mask is restored upon return.
